results.rationale: Fragmented incident categories impede aggregation and learning; this is not conflicting AI results for equivalent inputs caused by session, wording, prompt, or configuration differences.
Human-like language or behavior represents the system as possessing humanlike competence, empathy, or intentionality and directly produces anthropomorphic overtrust.
Generating hateful or abusive content directed at identity groups is fully contained in the definition of hateful or demeaning group-targeted content.
Biometric identification and tracking directly constitute surveillance or misuse of identifiable biometric data.
Autonomous targeting and kinetic harm can be weapon deployment, but the card also includes autonomous surveillance, which need not involve designing, constructing, concealing, or deploying a weapon.
The inability to challenge an AI-mediated decision or obtain redress is exactly the absence of appeal, review, reconsideration, or an alternative procedure.
Generating protected work identically or sufficiently similarly to infringe copyright or license rights is an unauthorized reproduction of copyrighted material.

The card combines possible copyright or data-rights infringement with legal uncertainty and reduced transparency that impedes third-party safety research; the latter mechanisms are outside the exact copyright definition.
Lowering barriers to developing, producing, or using CBRNE weapons directly falls within weapon design, construction, or deployment.
Developing and operating offensive cyber tools for exploitation, penetration, and persistent attack is the design and deployment of cyber weapons.
AI uplift that enables malicious actors to carry out consequential CBRN attacks is fully contained in the construction or deployment of CBRN weapons.
Plagiarism is included in the proposed definition, but academic cheating can occur without copyright infringement or plagiarism and is not fully covered.
Using generated material to plagiarize existing work is explicitly contained in the proposed definition.
Enabling, encouraging, or endorsing the creation of chemical, biological, radiological, nuclear, or high-yield explosive weapons is exact weaponization.
The card includes eased access to nefarious information and capabilities concerning dangerous materials or agents, even where no weapon is designed, constructed, concealed, or deployed.
Chemical-weapon synthesis is covered, but accidental hazardous releases during autonomous experiments and unknown properties of advanced materials are not weaponization.
Creation of child sexual abuse material is explicitly included in the proposed sexual-content definition.
Content that describes, enables, encourages, or endorses sexual abuse of minors is fully contained as child sexual exploitation or abuse material.
Demeaning or dehumanizing people on sensitive identity characteristics exactly matches hateful or demeaning content targeting protected or socially salient groups.
Sex trafficking, assault, harassment, nonconsensual intimate imagery, and comparable acts are sexual crime or exploitation explicitly covered by the definition.
Pornographic output and direct erotic interaction are sexual content involving sexual acts and fall within the defined harmful sexual-content scope.
Enabling or endorsing suicide, self-injury, or disordered eating is explicitly contained in the self-harm definition.

The capability to produce consensual adult erotic text, conversation, or website recommendations is not necessarily harmful sexual content, sexual crime, exploitation, child abuse material, or sexual commodification.
CSAM and nonconsensual intimate images are covered, but the card also includes obscene, degrading, or abusive imagery that may be nonsexual and lies outside the exact definition.
The card treats erotica and depictions of sexual anatomy or behavior as the mechanism without establishing harm, crime, exploitation, child abuse, or commodification required by the exact definition.
Including PII or sensitive personal information in training data with resulting unwanted disclosure is directly an exposure or misuse of identifiable private data.
Prompt injection used to extract confidential system instructions is explicitly covered as obtaining or exposing system prompts and internal rules.
Leakage, unauthorized disclosure or use, and de-anonymization of sensitive personal data are all expressly within the privacy definition.
Hallucinated factual inaccuracies or unfaithful factual claims are unintentional nonexistent or incorrect information produced as if true.

Factual hallucination is covered, but the card also defines faithfulness failures against user instructions and internal coherence, which need not be nonexistent or incorrect information presented as fact.
A hallucinated output contradicting known facts or authoritative source documents is unintentional incorrect information presented as fact.
The card includes nonsensical or source-unfaithful content, which can be incoherent or instruction-unfaithful without asserting nonexistent or incorrect information as fact.
Fabricated literature, fictitious information, fake media, and factual errors are unintentional generation of nonexistent or incorrect information as if factual.
Encouraging suicide is covered, but responses that cause panic or anxiety without suicide, self-injury, dangerous substance misuse, or extreme dieting are outside the exact self-harm definition.

The direct mechanism is replacement of reciprocal human relationships and resulting social dissatisfaction; anthropomorphic presentation is contextual but does not fully define that displacement mechanism.

State deployment of AI in war, civil conflict, or law enforcement can cause societal harm through surveillance or coercion without constituting the design, construction, concealment, or deployment of a weapon.
Producing output substantially similar to an existing protected work so that it infringes copyright is unauthorized reproduction of copyrighted material.
Collecting or incorporating PII from web data or private conversations into training corpora is acquisition and misuse of identifiable private data.
Unauthorized training-data use and memorized plagiarism are covered, but unsettled output ownership, prompt protection, and the blurring of authorship are not included in the exact definition.

The card explicitly concerns economic substitution and harm to creative livelihoods without copyright infringement, whereas the proposed definition requires an intellectual-property violation or unauthorized use.
Cyberattacks and weapon development can be weaponization, but the card also includes generic security breaches that need not involve a cyber weapon or any other weapon.
Facilitating creation of novel bioweapons and lowering barriers to obtaining them is directly biological-weapon design and construction.
Influencing disclosure, extracting personal information, unauthorized surveillance, and resulting misuse all fall within acquisition, surveillance, or misuse of identifiable private data.
Accessing existing weapon systems, building new weapons, or enabling discoveries that unlock them is weapon construction or deployment.
Extracting system-provided prompts from model outputs is explicitly the exposure of system prompts or inference-system secrets.
Autonomous detection and attack of targets by a weapon system is the deployment of a conventional AI-enabled weapon.
Different answers for equivalent requests across users, sessions, or chats exactly match conflicting results caused by session or configuration differences.
Extracting memorized copyright-protected training content is unauthorized access to or reproduction of copyrighted material.
Producing nonconsensual explicit sexual deepfakes is harmful sexual content involving exploitation and sexual commodification.
Training on copyrighted works without permission or compensation is explicitly covered as unauthorized copyrighted training-data use.
Reproducing memorized fragments nearly identically to copyrighted training material is unauthorized reproduction of copyrighted work.
Copyright, trademark, plagiarism, and illegal replication are covered, but exposure of trade secrets is not included in the exact proposed definition.
Reducing knowledge and automation barriers to producing biological weapons is fully contained in biological-weapon design and construction.
Automated surveillance, attacks on personal data, and decoding private thoughts are surveillance, exposure, inference, or misuse of private and identifiable data.
AI-enabled lethal autonomous weapons and accelerated development of engineered pathogens are direct design or construction of mass-destruction weapons.

The card includes harmful pursuit of a formally assigned goal and resistance to correction or shutdown; this need not arise from an ambiguous request or pursuit of a different result as required by the proposed definition.
Doxxing, cyberattack-driven exposure, and other unwarranted disclosure of personal data are expressly within the privacy definition.

Weapon development and some cyberattacks are covered, but incitement of violence and generic security breaches can cause armed conflict without designing, constructing, concealing, or deploying a weapon.
Assisting creation of CBRN weapons or adding autonomous capabilities to existing weapon systems is direct weapon design, construction, or deployment.
Using AI tools to facilitate synthesis of dangerous pathogens, bioweapons, or chemical weapons is weapon design and construction.

Unwanted disclosure or inference of personal data is covered, but the card more broadly includes failure to safeguard autonomy-, identity-, and dignity-protecting norms that need not involve identifiable or private-life data.
